# UC-20.3.2 · Software Audit Readiness Reporting

## Description

Audit-ready evidence of installs, purchases, and usage reduces true-up penalties and speeds vendor true-up negotiations.

## Value

Audit-ready evidence of installs, purchases, and usage reduces true-up penalties and speeds vendor true-up negotiations.

## Implementation

Normalize discovery data from endpoints and purchase records from procurement. Refresh entitlements from contract system. Dashboard shows install vs entitlement gap by publisher. Export CSV for auditor quarterly.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Flexera / Snow SAM export, ServiceNow SAM, Splunk Universal Forwarder inventory.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=license:usage`, `sourcetype=inventory:software`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Normalize discovery data from endpoints and purchase records from procurement. Refresh entitlements from contract system. Dashboard shows install vs entitlement gap by publisher. Export CSV for auditor quarterly.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=software (sourcetype="license:usage" OR sourcetype="inventory:software")
| eval publisher=coalesce(publisher,vendor), edition=coalesce(edition,product_name)
| stats dc(host) as install_count sum(entitlement_count) as purchased by publisher, edition
| eval gap=install_count-purchased
| where gap>0 OR isnull(purchased)
| table publisher, edition, install_count, purchased, gap
```

Understanding this SPL

**Software Audit Readiness Reporting** — Audit-ready evidence of installs, purchases, and usage reduces true-up penalties and speeds vendor true-up negotiations.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=license:usage`, `sourcetype=inventory:software`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Flexera / Snow SAM export, ServiceNow SAM, Splunk Universal Forwarder inventory. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: software; **sourcetype**: license:usage, inventory:software. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=software, sourcetype="license:usage". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **publisher** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by publisher, edition** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **gap** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where gap>0 OR isnull(purchased)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Software Audit Readiness Reporting**): table publisher, edition, install_count, purchased, gap


Step 3 — Validate
compare with ServiceNow ITAM; compare with the cloud billing console for the same scope, accounts, and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (gap by title), Bar chart (over-deployed publishers), Single value (total gap count).

## SPL

```spl
index=software (sourcetype="license:usage" OR sourcetype="inventory:software")
| eval publisher=coalesce(publisher,vendor), edition=coalesce(edition,product_name)
| stats dc(host) as install_count sum(entitlement_count) as purchased by publisher, edition
| eval gap=install_count-purchased
| where gap>0 OR isnull(purchased)
| table publisher, edition, install_count, purchased, gap
```

## Visualization

Table (gap by title), Bar chart (over-deployed publishers), Single value (total gap count).
